teacher_themes.label: Classroom Mental Health Literacy & Early Identification
teacher_themes.explanation: Districts want teachers to recognize signs of student mental health struggles, including anxiety, depression, and suicide warning signs, and understand how MH treatment supports classroom success.

teacher_themes.label: Tier 1 & Classroom-Based Intervention Tools
teacher_themes.explanation: Several districts requested practical, downloadable resources for teachers including de-escalation tools, sticker charts, classroom handouts, and a dedicated teacher landing page.

teacher_themes.label: Supporting Students with IEPs & Special Needs
teacher_themes.explanation: Interest in targeted teacher content for students with IEPs, behavior plans, and special needs, including 1-pagers and specific guidance.

teacher_themes.label: Professional Development / PD Day Sessions
teacher_themes.explanation: Districts expressed interest in structured PD sessions for teachers, ranging from 1-hour workshops to full back-to-school day presentations led by Daybreak.

teacher_themes.label: Culturally Responsive & Emotional Storytelling Resources
teacher_themes.explanation: Request for resources that humanize mental health for teachers, including behavioral success stories, cultural diversity content, and FAQs to reduce stigma.

items: Create new staff files due to significant staffing changes
Capture full outstanding items list; call was initial test/renewal conversation
Clarify plan of attack for care requests received during onboarding
Send data summary to district contact
Lock down Dr. Alvarado introduction
Utilize teachers and principals if RIFs impact mental health staff next year
Confirm last day for referrals within two weeks
Develop family surveys for 2026–27 school year
Send parent outreach content to leadership folder
Explore possible uninsured upsell opportunity
Complete onboarding for 1 remaining student
Explain close-out and enrollment deactivation process to district contact
Deliver updated staff file by July
Confirm training dates
Submit staff file

feedback_themes.theme: Referral conversion, no-show management, and care request follow-through
examples: Kipp Nor Cal: 57 referrals but only 7 in care; dashboard monitoring is a critical BTS need.
Horizon Charters: Requests better notification of no-shows and clearer two-session no-show discharge policy.
Fulton County: Frustrated that 6 students enrolled but only 1 received service in reasonable timeframe.

feedback_themes.theme: Appointment notifications and communication transparency with school staff
examples: Snowline Joint: Staff want easy notification of appointments during school hours to prepare space and schedule.
Jefferson County: All service communications must be triaged through counselors; scheduling coordination needs tightening.
Belmont-Redwood Shores: Requests more transparency on self-funded plan denials under CYBHI.

feedback_themes.theme: Positive partnership experience and growing utilization
examples: Chicago Heights 170: First year described as good; Daybreak seen as a viable and helpful resource.
Lammersville Joint: Looking forward to year two with positive momentum.
Escalon: Referrals increased significantly compared to previous year; district is pleased.

feedback_themes.theme: Marketing, outreach materials, and expanding program awareness
examples: Adelanto Elementary: Marketing content needs to feel more family-friendly and approachable.
Freeport 145: Year two focus on more advertising and getting the word out to families.
Redding School District: Low utilization attributed to late start; expects higher engagement next year.
